channel.title: SQLite on Margrop Blog
channel.description: Recent content in SQLite on Margrop Blog

item.title: Do Not Just Copy QingLong Into BaiHu: A Real Safe Migration Drill
item.description:
Bottom line first

Migrating from QingLong Panel to BaiHu Panel is not a directory-copy exercise. I ran a real migration drill in an isolated Docker environment: a fresh QingLong instance with sample environment variables, scripts, labels, and cron jobs; then a fresh BaiHu instance; then an actual conversion and verification pass.

The reliable migration unit is not the whole QingLong data directory. It is a set of assets: script files, environment variables, scheduled tasks, tags, enabled/disabled state, and task-variable relations. QingLong uses numeric IDs; BaiHu uses string IDs and relation tables. QingLong commands such as task daily_check.py also need to become executable BaiHu commands such as python /app/data/scripts/qinglong-migrated/daily_check.py.

This article contains no full private IP address, private registry address, host name, token, cookie, or production secret. The screenshots come from a disposable lab, and the variables are redacted sample data.
